changes.before: Books, boards, and specimen jars filled the stone room.
changes.after: Specimen jars crowded the walls around the boards.

changes.before: Kel'Thuzad raised his head as another professional might mark a late arrival to an unpleasant meeting.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad raised his head as another professional might note a late arrival to an unpleasant meeting.

changes.before: Worse: she stood like she belonged there.
changes.after: She stood three strides off the board, staff down, settled into the room instead of braced against it.

changes.before: A weakness she could not ignore.
changes.after: Jaina held still instead of reaching for the staff.

changes.before: Sylvanas had her answer: Jaina was still useful and dangerous, but Kel'Thuzad could keep her there longer by feeding her structure than safety allowed.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad could hold her with a board and a question longer than chains would have.

changes.before: Waiting to learn whether she still belonged on the same side of it.
changes.after: Waiting to see where Sylvanas would put her next.

changes.before: It still worked, and Sylvanas felt the cost.
changes.after: It worked, but she had checked before she moved.

changes.before: He gave one last useful fact before he folded.
changes.after: Even dying, he gave them a direction.

changes.before: The cup had gone cold beside pages worked over long enough to grind chalk into the grain.
changes.after: The cup sat cold beside pages dusted white from use.

changes.before: Good. Any explanation here would sound like a plea, and Jaina left it unsaid.
changes.after: Good. Jaina left it at that and moved.

changes.before: But the ordering mind was gone.
changes.after: Without Kel'Thuzad at the center, the wards were already slipping their pattern.

changes.before: Jaina kept pace easily enough, but the quiet around her felt heavier now.
changes.after: Jaina kept pace easily enough, but she gave Sylvanas nothing beyond the steps themselves.

changes.before: They fell back into line by habit, but the old spacing never came back.
changes.after: They fell back into line by habit.
